customer_anxiety_profile: Low anxiety, high information-orientation. Priyank is a researcher — he wants to understand options and costs before committing. He is not hostile or skeptical of solar; he is methodical. He will respond well to a closer who leads with his specific numbers rather than a generic pitch.
unresolved_concerns: Government subsidies and tax credits — Priyank raised this in his opening statement and it was never addressed or bridged forward.
Cost and financing options — Priyank's stated goal is to understand 'how much it's gonna cost' before deciding.
Wife has not been confirmed for the appointment — she is on the title and has not been included in the scheduling.
sensitive_topics_raised: EV charging load — Priyank is already doing his own math on home vs. workplace charging split. He is cost-conscious and analytical about his energy use.
Timing uncertainty — Priyank explicitly said he is 'not sure' if he wants to proceed now or in the future. He is not a ready buyer; he is an evaluator.
exact_promises_rep_made: Appointment booked for the 27th at 2PM (Monday afternoon).
Confirmation text with date and time will be sent once the bill is received.
Text message with rep's email was sent during the call for bill submission.
closer_should_know: This appointment is contingent on bill receipt — Randy did not send a confirmed calendar invite, only a promise to confirm once the bill arrives. Priyank is going on vacation the week before the appointment. If the bill has not been received and the appointment not confirmed before the vacation, the no-show risk is high. Closer or floor coordinator should verify bill receipt and send confirmation before Priyank leaves. Also: Priyank's 'check out the house' language at 07:27 suggests he may expect an in-person visit — closer should clarify the phone format at the top of the call.
